recipes.title: Beef Noodle Soup
recipes.description: Taiwanese braised beef and noodle soup in a soy-based broth with star anise, chillies and bok choy.
steps: Parboil beef shank and tendon for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Saute onion, ginger and garlic in oil; add doubanjiang and cook until red and fragrant.
Add tomato wedges and cook until softened.
Pour in soy sauce, dark soy, Shaoxing wine, rock sugar and water.
Add tied spice sachet of star anise, cinnamon, peppercorns and bay leaves.
Add beef and tendon; simmer covered for 2.5 hours until very tender.
Cook noodles in boiling water and divide between bowls with blanched bok choy.
Slice beef, top noodles with broth, beef and tendon, then garnish with scallions.

recipes.title: Bubble Tea
recipes.description: Taiwanese tea drink with chewy tapioca pearls, brown sugar and milk, served cold over ice.
steps: Boil tapioca pearls in plenty of water for 25 minutes until chewy and translucent.
Drain pearls and toss with half the brown sugar and a splash of water.
Steep black tea bags in hot water for 5 minutes, then remove and stir in remaining sugar.
Cool the sweetened tea fully.
Spoon brown sugar pearls into tall glasses, swirl up the sides for a marbled effect.
Add ice, pour in tea and milk to fill, then stir before drinking with wide straws.

recipes.title: Lu Rou Fan
recipes.description: Taiwanese braised pork belly rice bowl with five-spice, soy and rock sugar over steamed rice.
steps: Dice pork belly into small 1 cm cubes.
Fry shallots in oil until deeply golden, then remove and reserve.
Sear pork belly in the shallot oil until browned and fat is rendered.
Add garlic, then pour in soy sauce, dark soy, Shaoxing, rock sugar, five-spice, star anise and pepper.
Add water and the fried shallots, then bring to a simmer.
Cover and braise for 1 hour until pork is tender.
Hard-boil eggs, peel and add to the pot for the last 30 minutes.
Spoon braised pork over steamed rice with a halved egg and pickled mustard greens.

recipes.title: Gua Bao
recipes.description: Taiwanese steamed buns folded around braised pork belly, pickled mustard greens, peanuts and cilantro.
steps: Mix flour, sugar, yeast, salt, milk and oil into a smooth dough; knead 10 minutes and rise 1 hour.
Divide dough into 8 ovals, brush with oil and fold each in half over baking paper.
Steam buns for 10 minutes over high heat until pillowy.
Sear pork belly slices, then braise in soy sauce, rock sugar, five-spice, star anise and ginger for 1 hour.
Crush peanuts with sugar to make a sweet powder.
Open each warm bun, place pork inside, top with pickled mustard greens, peanut sugar and cilantro.

recipes.title: Oyster Omelette
recipes.description: Taiwanese street snack of oysters in a starchy egg pancake topped with sweet-sour pink sauce.
steps: Whisk sweet potato starch with water to make a slurry.
Mix tomato sauce, miso, sugar, vinegar and cornstarch with a little water; simmer until pink and glossy.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan and place oysters in a circle.
Pour starch slurry over the oysters, swirling to a thin pancake.
Crack eggs over the top and pierce yolks gently.
When eggs are nearly set, scatter chopped bok choy and minced garlic on top.
Flip and cook briefly, then slide onto a plate and pour over the pink sauce.

recipes.title: Stinky Tofu
recipes.description: Taiwanese fermented tofu, deep-fried until crisp and served with pickled cabbage and chilli sauce.
steps: Slice cabbage and carrot, then salt for 30 minutes; drain and toss with vinegar and sugar to pickle.
Pat stinky tofu very dry.
Heat oil to 180C in a wok or deep pan.
Carefully lower tofu into the oil and fry 5 minutes until skin is crisp and golden.
Drain on paper towels.
Whisk soy sauce, minced garlic and chilli sauce for the dip.
Slit each tofu open, stuff with pickled cabbage and top with cilantro and chilli sauce.

recipes.title: Three Cup Chicken
recipes.description: Taiwanese chicken braised in equal parts soy sauce, rice wine and sesame oil with garlic and basil.
steps: Heat sesame oil in a clay pot over low heat.
Add thick ginger slices and fry slowly until edges curl and dry out.
Add whole peeled garlic cloves and dried chillies; toast briefly.
Add bone-in chicken pieces and sear until golden.
Pour in soy sauce, rice wine and rock sugar; bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes until chicken is cooked and sauce reduces to a glaze.
Stir in a generous handful of Thai basil and cover off the heat for 1 minute before serving.

recipes.title: Pineapple Cake
recipes.description: Taiwanese buttery shortbread filled with sweet-tart pineapple jam, pressed into rectangular molds.
steps: Grate pineapple and squeeze out the juice; reserve flesh for jam.
Cook pineapple flesh, sugar and maltose over low heat for 1 hour, stirring until thick, dark and dry.
Cool the jam fully and roll into 12 g balls.
Cream butter and powdered sugar, then mix in eggs and yolks.
Fold in flour, milk powder and salt to make a soft dough; chill 30 minutes.
Portion 25 g of dough, flatten and wrap around a pineapple ball, then press into rectangular molds.
Bake at 170C for 20 minutes, flipping halfway, until lightly golden; cool fully before unmolding.

recipes.title: Scallion Pancake
recipes.description: Taiwanese flaky pan-fried flatbread layered with scallions and oil, crisp on the outside and tender within.
steps: Pour boiling water into flour and stir, then add cold water and salt; knead until smooth.
Rest dough for 30 minutes.
Divide into 4 balls and roll each one tissue-thin.
Brush with sesame oil, sprinkle with chopped scallions, salt and pepper.
Roll up tightly into a long rope, then coil into a snail shape and rest 15 minutes.
Roll each coil into a 20 cm pancake.
Pan-fry in vegetable oil over medium heat for 3 minutes per side until deep golden and flaky.
Cut into wedges and serve hot.
